_schema: Equipment. 6 condition subsystems (1-100; a template value of 0 means NOT APPLICABLE to that machine — the baler has no engine, the truck has no hydraulics — and is excluded from all condition math). Mechanic sees all applicable subsystems; other backgrounds see summary_state from the worst (fine >60, worn >35, rough >15, failing <=15). Condition DRIVES gameplay: breakdown odds, breakdown severity, field yield, and work cost. slot = which working role the machine fills (Roy's floor swaps within a slot, trade-in mandatory). dealer_stock = what Carver Equipment has on the floor. ALL NUMBERS PLACEHOLDER — economy re-derivation locks them (2026-07-06 freeze lift).

breakdown_profile._note: Shared severity table. weight picks the tier at breakdown; worse condition biases the pick toward the costly end (code). 'keep running' amplifies condition_hit and, after 3 ignores, forces the next failure to expensive. cost_dealer = Roy fixes it today; cost_self_parts = Mechanic self-fix; downtime_days = cost of letting it sit.

dealer_stock.name: 2018 Harvestall (one owner)
dealer_stock.roy_line: One owner, church on Sundays. I'd run it myself.

dealer_stock.name: Field-ready round baler
dealer_stock.roy_line: It bales. That's the whole review.
